objectStorageName.markdownDescription:
Object storage logical name

Identifies the [Object Storage](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage#/objectstorageaccessclassname) containing the blob to index (e.g., AWS).

container.markdownDescription:
Name of the input Object Storage container

Specifies the Object Storage container (e.g., AWS S3 bucket) containing the blob (e.g., log file) to index.

object.markdownDescription:
Name of the object storage blob to index

Specifies the blob (e.g., log file) name within [indexReadContainer](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexreadcontainer) to index.

extractMessage.markdownDescription:
Use extractor for inner message

Specifies whether to extract an inner field from the entire event json to use as the base for constructing the TenXObject. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: false)

messageField.markdownDescription:
Message field name

Name of the actual message field in the event json to use to construct the TenXObject, used only if [indexReadExtractMessage](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexreadextractmessage) is true. (Default: log)

printProgress.markdownDescription:
Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console

Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console. This value is commonly used when testing an integration to a remote endpoint. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

container.markdownDescription:
Name of target index container

Specifies the storage container (e.g., AWS S3 bucket) to output [TenXTemplate Filters](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#tenxtemplate-filters)  (e.g., TenXTemplates and Bloom filters).

target.markdownDescription:
Logical name identifying the origin of 'indexReadObject'

Specifies a logical name to store index objects produced for [indexReadObject](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexreadobject) under. This name commonly specifies the app which generated the events enclosed within this blob (e.g. `acme-client`).

byteRange.markdownDescription:
Max byte range size to index 'indexReadObject'

Controls the chunk size in which to index the target object. For example, if the target object is 1GB and this value is `2MB`, index the object in 2MB segments to ensure matching queries can retrieve chunks vs. all of it unnecessarily. To learn more see: [byte range fetches](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/s3-optimizing-performance-best-practices/use-byte-range-fetches.html) (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

resolution.markdownDescription:
Index time window resolution

Controls the index time range resolution. For example, setting this to `1min` means that queries to the index at time ranges greater than 1min (e.g. 15min) will not fetch byte ranges outside the time frame unnecessarily.  The lower this value is, the greater the output index size will be.  This value should satisfy the minimum resolution for querying the index. For example, if queries to the index are in 5-minute increments:  ``` yaml query:   filter:     from: $=now("-5m")     to: $=now() ```  Setting this value to `5min` will create the most efficient index. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

accuracy.markdownDescription:
Bloom filter accuracy of index objects.

Controls the accuracy of bloom filter [TenXTemplate Filters](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#tenxtemplate-filters). The index output stream produces a list of [Bloom filters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom_filter) for each [indexWriteResolution](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexwriteresolution) and [indexWriteByteRange](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexwritebyterange) combination of the target blob. [Query](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/) inputs utilize these filter objects to rule out byte ranges where the query criteria are known NOT to match.  For example, if a target blob weighing 10MB contains events whose timestamps range from the beginning of the hour to 3min later, and [indexWriteResolution](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexwriteresolution) is set to `1min` and [indexWriteByteRange](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexwritebyterange) is set to `2Mb`, up to 6 ranges are indexed separately, where the [templateHash](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+templateHash) and [vars](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+vars) members of each TenXObject are within that range are added to a list of bloom filters whose accuracy must not fall below this value. The greater the accuracy, the greater the list of filters is created.  The [query](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/query/) input uses Bloom filters to evaluate whether their corresponding byte ranges contain target search terms with an accuracy (i.e., chance of false positive) set by these values. In other words, if this value is 95, there is a 5% chance that a byte range that does NOT contain target terms is fetched and scanned. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

templateMergeInterval.markdownDescription:
Merge template interval

Specifies the interval to wait between template merge operation. Each index operation stores output [TenXTemplate](https://doc.log10x.com/run/template/) objects in the [indexWriteContainer](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexwritecontainer). Index operations merge templates files into a single file periodically, with the period interval set by this value. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

objectStorageArgs.markdownDescription:
Custom Object storage args

Custom arguments passed as a map to the constructor of the underlying [object storage](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/objectStorage/index/#indexobjectstoragename). This list is expected to hold pairs of key values (e.g., args: [key1, value1, key2, value2])
